heading: Duties of Commission; publication of guidelines; report to Congress
label: 4103 Duties of Commission; publication of guidelines; report to Congress
notes: Amendments1990—Subsec. (a)(4), (6). , (2), substituted “recommend” for “suggest”.Pub. L. 101–609, § 4(a)(1)Subsec. (a)(9), (10). –(5), added pars. (9) and (10).Pub. L. 101–609, § 4(a)(3)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “Not later than January 31 of each year, the Commission shall—Pub. L. 101–609, § 4(b)“(1) publish a statement of goals and objectives with respect to Arctic research to guide the Interagency Committee established under in the performance of its duties; andsection 4106 of this title“(2) submit to the President and to the Congress a report describing the activities and accomplishments of the Commission during the immediately preceding fiscal year.”

children.text: develop and recommend an integrated national Arctic research policy;

in cooperation with the Interagency Arctic Research Policy Committee established under , assist in establishing a national Arctic research program plan to implement the Arctic research policy;section 4106 of this title
facilitate cooperation between the Federal Government and State and local governments with respect to Arctic research;
review Federal research programs in the Arctic and recommend improvements in coordination among programs;
recommend methods to improve logistical planning and support for Arctic research as may be appropriate and in accordance with the findings and purposes of this chapter;
recommend methods for improving efficient sharing and dissemination of data and information on the Arctic among interested public and private institutions;
offer other recommendations and advice to the Interagency Committee established under as it may find appropriate;section 4106 of this title
cooperate with the Governor of the State of Alaska and with agencies and organizations of that State which the Governor may designate with respect to the formulation of Arctic research policy;
recommend to the Interagency Committee the means for developing international scientific cooperation in the Arctic; and

not later than , and every 2 years thereafter, publish a statement of goals and objectives with respect to Arctic research to guide the Interagency Committee established under in the performance of its duties.January 31, 1991section 4106 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: Not later than January 31 of each year, the Commission shall submit to the President and to the Congress a report describing the activities and accomplishments of the Commission during the immediately preceding fiscal year.
